title: podman 1.7.0 take ages to list images, after upgrading to Fedora 31.
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
After uprading from Fedora 30 to Fedora 31, now `podman images` command. takes a lot of time to list all my images.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Upgrade to Fedora 31

2. Run `podman images`

3. Wait ~ 5 minutes.

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[jtudelag@jtudelag-t480s ~]$ time podman --log-level debug images

```
podman version
Version:            1.7.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.5
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

```
rpm -q podman
podman-1.7.0-2.fc31.x86_64
```

comments.body: For the debug logs - are those printed all at once, or gradually as Podman runs? How many total images do you have installed?

I have 115 images in total.  
The pair of lines:  `parsed reference....`   & `exporting opaque data...` are printed sequentually in pairs. It seems the output of a loop ;).
Is it accidently getting the size of the images?

Images doesn't seem to support a `size` parameter, so I don't think it would be calculating it.

I can't replicate this locally, even with a large amount of images pulled. Wiping your existing storage by removing `~/.local/share/containers/storage` might fix this but will remove all your existing containers and images.
@jtudelag, are you still seeing the issue?

Hey @vrothberg , no, it happened after the upgrade from f30 to f31. I did run a container with podman, and after that the error was gone. (I had other problems with cgroups v2 and rootless containers) but that was a different story ;).
Thanks! I am closing the issue then.

Hey @vrothberg, this is happening again in F31 with podman 1.8.1....
````podman version
Version:            1.8.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```
Thanks for reaching out again, @jtudelag! We have a new issue with the same symptoms (https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5631). Let's chat over there.

Running `podman images`, In this print stuck in an infinite loop.
